chapter-09-split-01-q02.explanation: Litmus turns blue in basic/alkaline solutions.

chapter-09-split-01-q04.explanation: NH4+ is the conjugate acid of weak base NH3 → acidic hydrolysis.

chapter-09-split-01-q05.explanation: HCl fully ionizes in water (strong acid).

chapter-09-split-01-q07.explanation: Carbonate ion hydrolyzes to give OH- → basic solution.

chapter-09-split-01-q11.explanation: Salt commonly forms from acid–base neutralization.

chapter-09-split-01-q12.explanation: HCl gas dissolves producing hydrochloric acid solution.

chapter-09-split-01-q13.explanation: Litmus turns red in acid and blue in base.

chapter-09-split-01-q14.explanation: NaCl from strong acid & strong base → neutral salt.

chapter-09-split-01-q15.explanation: NaOH is used for neutralization and titrations.

chapter-09-split-01-q16.explanation: HCl, HNO3 and H2SO4 are all strong (fully dissociate) strong mineral acids.

chapter-09-split-01-q17.correctOption: Resists pH change on addition of small amounts of acid/base
chapter-09-split-01-q17.explanation: Buffer resists pH change by neutralizing added acid/base.

chapter-09-split-01-q18.explanation: Indicators show a sudden color change near equivalence point.

chapter-09-split-01-q19.explanation: F- is conjugate base of weak acid HF → solution basic.

chapter-09-split-01-q20.explanation: Diprotic acid neutralizes with two moles of base giving sulfate salt and water.

chapter-09-split-01-q21.explanation: Sulfur dioxide and nitrogen oxides form acids in atmosphere.

chapter-09-split-01-q22.correctOption: Colorless in acid, pink in base
chapter-09-split-01-q22.explanation: Phenolphthalein is colorless below pH ~8.2 and pink above ~10.

chapter-09-split-01-q23.explanation: Acetate ion hydrolyzes producing OH- → basic solution.

chapter-09-split-01-q24.explanation: Aluminium oxide reacts with both acids and bases → amphoteric.
